theorems.name: Pythagorean Theorem
theorems.proof_md: For right triangles, compare areas by geometric rearrangement.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, College Algebra 2e (2022), Ch. 8.2 Right Triangle Trigonometry, pp. 582-589.

theorems.name: Binomial Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Expand product and count selections by combinations.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Precalculus 2e (2021), Ch. 5.4 Binomial Theorem, pp. 392-399.

theorems.name: AM-GM Inequality
theorems.proof_md: Use (sqrt(a)-sqrt(b))^2 >= 0.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Algebra and Trigonometry 2e (2021), Ch. 2 Inequalities, pp. 133-141.

theorems.name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
theorems.proof_md: Consider non-negativity of ||ta-b||^2 and optimize t.
theorems.refs: HELM Consortium Workbook 8, Cauchy-Schwarz, pp. 31-35.

theorems.name: Triangle Inequality
theorems.proof_md: Square both sides and use positivity of inner products.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Precalculus 2e (2021), Ch. 9 Vectors, pp. 666-671.

theorems.name: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
theorems.statement_latex: Every integer n>1 has unique prime factorization.
theorems.proof_md: Existence by induction, uniqueness via Euclid's lemma.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Contemporary Mathematics (2024), Ch. 3 Number Theory, pp. 177-184.

theorems.name: Euclid's Lemma
theorems.proof_md: Use Bezout identity and divisibility closure.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Contemporary Mathematics (2024), Ch. 3 Number Theory, pp. 184-187.

theorems.name: Chinese Remainder Theorem
theorems.statement_latex: Pairwise coprime moduli yield unique solution modulo product.
theorems.proof_md: Construct solution with modular inverses.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Contemporary Mathematics (2024), Ch. 3.4 Modular Arithmetic, pp. 196-204.

theorems.name: Fermat Little Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Use permutation of nonzero residues modulo p.
theorems.conditions: p prime, p does not divide a
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Contemporary Mathematics (2024), Ch. 3.5 Number Theory Applications, pp. 204-210.

theorems.name: Euler Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Generalize Fermat via reduced residue system.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Contemporary Mathematics (2024), Ch. 3.5 Number Theory Applications, pp. 210-214.

theorems.name: Bayes Theorem
theorems.proof_md: From definition of conditional probability.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics 2e (2023), Ch. 3.6 Bayes Rule, pp. 181-190.

theorems.name: Law of Total Probability
theorems.proof_md: Partition sample space by mutually exclusive events.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics 2e (2023), Ch. 3.5 Conditional Probability, pp. 170-181.

theorems.name: Central Limit Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Use characteristic functions or Lindeberg approach.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics 2e (2023), Ch. 7.2 Central Limit Theorem, pp. 404-415.

theorems.name: Weak Law of Large Numbers
theorems.statement_latex: Sample mean converges in probability to expectation.
theorems.proof_md: Apply Chebyshev inequality to sample mean variance.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics 2e (2023), Ch. 7 Sampling Distributions, pp. 394-403.

theorems.name: Taylor Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Repeated integration or mean value theorem.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 2 (2022), Ch. 6.3 Taylor and Maclaurin Series, pp. 446-462.

theorems.name: Mean Value Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Apply Rolle theorem to adjusted function.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 1 (2022), Ch. 4.2 Mean Value Theorem, pp. 300-309.

theorems.name: Rolle Theorem
theorems.proof_md: Use extreme value theorem and interior extremum.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 1 (2022), Ch. 4.2 Mean Value Theorem, pp. 297-300.

theorems.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
theorems.statement_latex: Integral and derivative are inverse operations.
theorems.proof_md: Link accumulated area and derivative limits.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 1 (2022), Ch. 5.3 Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, pp. 376-389.

theorems.name: Green's Theorem
theorems.statement_latex: Line integral equals double integral of curl component.
theorems.proof_md: Partition region and cancel interior boundary terms.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 3 (2022), Ch. 6.4 Green's Theorem, pp. 501-515.

theorems.name: Stokes' Theorem
theorems.statement_latex: Surface integral of curl equals boundary line integral.
theorems.proof_md: Approximate surface by patches and sum boundary contributions.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 3 (2022), Ch. 6.8 Stokes' Theorem, pp. 561-575.

theorems.name: Divergence Theorem
theorems.statement_latex: Flux through boundary equals volume integral of divergence.
theorems.proof_md: Apply to boxes then pass to limits.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 3 (2022), Ch. 6.9 Divergence Theorem, pp. 576-589.

theorems.name: Rank-Nullity Theorem
theorems.statement_latex: dim Ker(T) + dim Im(T) = dim V
theorems.proof_md: Extend kernel basis to basis of V and map under T.
theorems.refs: Hefferon, Linear Algebra (free text, 4e), Ch. 3.2 Rank, pp. 93-101.

theorems.name: Spectral Theorem (Real Symmetric)
theorems.statement_latex: Real symmetric matrix is orthogonally diagonalizable.
theorems.proof_md: Use existence of orthonormal eigenbasis.
theorems.refs: Hefferon, Linear Algebra (free text, 4e), Ch. 8 Orthogonality and Diagonalization, pp. 301-317.

theorems.name: Jordan Curve Theorem
theorems.statement_latex: Simple closed curve partitions plane into inside and outside.
theorems.proof_md: Topological proof via winding numbers.
theorems.refs: CLP-4, Topology Supplement (UBC), Jordan Curve statement and discussion, pp. 12-18.

theorems.name: Inclusion-Exclusion Principle
theorems.proof_md: Count overlaps with alternating sums.
theorems.refs: OpenStax, Discrete Mathematics draft modules, Counting Principles, pp. 71-80.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, College Algebra 2e (2022), Ch. 2.4 Quadratic Equations, pp. 118-129.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, Contemporary Mathematics (2024), Ch. 8 Counting, pp. 480-489.

formulas.name: Slope Formula
formulas.meaning: Slope of line through two points.
formulas.refs: OpenStax, College Algebra 2e (2022), Ch. 3.1 Linear Functions, pp. 170-176.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, Precalculus 2e (2021), Ch. 8 Geometry Review, pp. 604-607.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, Precalculus 2e (2021), Ch. 8 Geometry Review, pp. 608-611.

formulas.name: Circle Circumference
formulas.refs: OpenStax, College Algebra 2e (2022), Ch. 5.5 Polar and Conic Review, pp. 356-358.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 1 (2022), Ch. 3.3 Derivative Rules, pp. 211-219.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 1 (2022), Ch. 3.4 Product and Quotient Rules, pp. 220-227.

formulas.name: Chain Rule
formulas.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 1 (2022), Ch. 3.5 Chain Rule, pp. 228-238.

formulas.name: Integration by Parts
formulas.latex: int u dv = uv - int v du
formulas.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 2 (2022), Ch. 1.2 Integration by Parts, pp. 24-33.

formulas.name: Gaussian Density
formulas.refs: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics 2e (2023), Ch. 6.2 Normal Distribution, pp. 329-338.

formulas.name: Variance Formula
formulas.refs: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics 2e (2023), Ch. 4.3 Variance and Standard Deviation, pp. 227-236.

formulas.name: Covariance Formula
formulas.refs: OpenIntro Statistics (4e, free), Ch. 7 Correlation and Regression, pp. 292-296.

formulas.refs: OpenIntro Statistics (4e, free), Ch. 7 Correlation and Regression, pp. 286-292.

formulas.name: Log Change of Base
formulas.latex: log_a b = ln b / ln a
formulas.refs: OpenStax, College Algebra 2e (2022), Ch. 4.3 Logarithmic Properties, pp. 291-295.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, College Algebra 2e (2022), Ch. 7.1 Systems of Equations and Matrices, pp. 501-508.

formulas.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 3 (2022), Ch. 2.4 Dot Product, pp. 170-178.

formulas.name: Cross Product Magnitude
formulas.meaning: Area of parallelogram spanned by vectors.
formulas.refs: OpenStax, Calculus Volume 3 (2022), Ch. 2.4 Cross Product, pp. 178-186.
